title: coordinator/permission: QueuedPermission docs promise a reply channel that doesn't exist
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/coordinator/permission.rs:20-26 (QueuedPermission)
## Violation
```rust
/// A permission request from a specific teammate, awaiting the
/// leader's decision. The reply channel lets the teammate's task
/// thread resume once the user decides.
pub struct QueuedPermission {
    pub teammate: TeammateId,
    pub tool_name: String,
    pub tool_args: String,
}
```

The doc comment claims there is a reply channel, but the struct has no oneshot, no `Sender`, no `Notify`, nothing the teammate could `.await` on. As specified, a teammate that calls `enqueue` has no way to learn the user's decision — the design literally cannot work without a reply path.

Phase 3 will discover this when wiring the event loop and either retrofit a channel onto every existing `QueuedPermission` constructor, or build a parallel out-of-band correlation map keyed by request id. Either path is a rework, and the gap is invisible to anyone reading the public API today.

## Mandated refactor
Add `reply: tokio::sync::oneshot::Sender<PermissionDecision>` (or equivalent) to `QueuedPermission` now, even unused. Update the FIFO and tests so the contract is enforced at compile time. If a oneshot is rejected on Phase 1 grounds, replace the doc-comment lie with an explicit `// TODO Phase 3: add reply channel — current shape cannot resume teammates`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
